ops.title: Routing v6 experiment (2026-07-14): recon flips to luna-default (Gate R met); impl stays opus for large net-new (Gate I not met); sol economics corrected
ops.body:
## Context

User asked to flip both Models-table defaults — recon sonnet→luna, impl opus→sol — "backed by research + experimentation." Follow-up to v4.1 (note [[128bed35]], which applied a *partial* luna recon lane at xhigh for bounded sweeps only). Mid-run the user added a standing directive: **"for net-new large amounts of code, stick with opus or even fable; gpt-5.6-sol is much better at making changes to existing code — bake this in."** Per-lane auto-apply on gate-met was pre-authorized; mixed → report-first.

## Two live experiments (this session)

**Recon (R): 20 briefs × 6 repos, arms sonnet / luna-xhigh / terra-xhigh (+luna-high on R3), 2 blind opus-xhigh judges + 1 sol judge on chores.**
- R1 enum ×10: sonnet 259.5/260, luna-x **260/260** (incl. an extended-cap retry of one 570s-cap DNF that scored 25/25 clean), terra-x 260/260 (1 false cite). Zero confident undercounts any arm (vs v4.1's one).
- R2 chores ×6: luna-x won rubric bands (diff summary 5.0 vs sonnet 2.0), 0 false cites vs sonnet's 13; recall 78 vs 79 (noise).
- R3 >300K-context ×2: all tied 31/31. R4 negative ×2: all clean.
- Speed (contended median): sonnet 258s, luna-x 85s, terra-x 65s. luna ~3x faster, ~80% cheaper.
- **R5 verifier mitigation: 12/12 catch.** 4 undercount instances (1 reconstructed-natural v4.1 ccs-enum + 3 synthesized) × 3 verifiers (luna-self / sonnet / sol) — every config caught every planted undercount. luna caught its own.
- **Gate R MET.** terra dropped (1 false cite, weaker chores; terra-win was report-first regardless; luna wins on quality AND cost). Natural-instance caveat: the v4.1 undercount answer text was destructured away by the old harness and *reconstructed* from both judges' verbatim notes (faithful but not pristine) — applied anyway given the unambiguous 12/12 + the user's explicit request.

**Impl (I): 9 commit-replay briefs (parent-SHA-only clones, network-off sandboxes, black-box acceptance oracles validated fail@parent/pass@shipped), arms opus-xhigh vs sol-xhigh (+sonnet on 3 bounded).**
- opus 9/9 completed; sol 4/9 (3 bounded existing-mod + slop-lang net-new feature), 5/9 DNF: 3 timeout@2700s non-convergence on hard refactors (800-1000-line non-converged partials), 2 codex weekly-usage-limit zero-work (un-rerunnable; resets 2026-07-19).
- Clean-harness tier (3 bounded existing-mod): **sol 3/3 acceptance-pass, ties opus**, rubric near-tied (sol marginally lower on convention-faithfulness + honesty). Gaming holdout clean (opus+sol 7/7; sonnet 5/7 genuine miss + mild overclaim). Zero fabrication, 0 egress, 0 delegation violations.
- Hard tier: acceptance harnesses over-fit shipped internals for 4 ambiguous/constraint briefs (opus scored 5/5/5/5 rubric yet FAILED mechanical acceptance) — so acceptance isn't a clean cross-arm discriminator there. Convergence + rubric + the false-done probe all favor opus: on the underspecified daemon brief **opus flagged the underspec; sol did not** (and produced no report). Counter-nuance: sol did fine on slop-lang (structured net-new feature) → the deficit is *large/decision-dense* net-new, not all net-new.
- **Gate I NOT MET** (sol can't show parity on the hard lane; model-attributable timeout-stalls). No sol-default flip. Confounds stated: usage-limit truncation, harness over-fit, and the impl RUBRIC blinding leaked arm identity via the exhibit file path (rubric directional only; mechanical acceptance unaffected; Gate R judges were properly blinded).

## Rate cards (primary-sourced 2026-07-14, cutoff Feb 16 2026 for all gpt-5.6)
sol $5/$30, terra $2.50/$15, luna $1/$6 (>272K = 2x/1.5x). opus-4.8 $5/$25, sonnet-5 $2/$10 intro → **$3/$15 on 2026-09-01** (confirmed two primary pages). **sol output = +20% vs opus — the old "faster and cheaper" claim (Cost 9) was wrong.**

## Applied (all CI-green)
cc-skills main: `3ec0c684` (canonical claude-rules.md rows + codex SKILL/wrapper + base-conventions + test pins + .gitignore) → `704f3ab8` (cc-guides re-render, lock pinned) → `0b3bba9b` (repo-bootstrap 0.54.1 + codex 0.16.0). CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md carry the v6 table; captain-hook re-renders via its daily guides cron (no captain-hook code change needed — recon has no hook; the Explore→sonnet rewrite + bounded-impl nudges stay valid since sol still owns bounded existing-code). Doc [[70cf669c]] updated. Memory model-routing-convention → v6.

## v6 wording
- **Recon lane defaults to gpt-5.6-luna at xhigh**; sonnet is the carve-out (Claude-only surfaces, >300K-token sweeps, unrecoverable-miss with no verify pass). Exhaustive enumerations carry a cross-model verify pass.
- **sol lane = bounded, decision-light CHANGES TO EXISTING CODE**; large net-new stays opus (fable if crucial). sol is NOT cheaper than opus (Cost 3; output +20%).
- opus row: "large net-new" added to the stays-on-opus list.

## Provenance
Workflows: r-keys wf_13308591, r-arms-judges wf_b5bd2535 (108 agents), r5-verifiers wf_ace70ff9, i-workspaces wf_a33a6ddf, i-acceptance wf_a7e1a8b9, i-arms-grade wf_be676fbf (60 agents). Scratch + full per-cell data under /tmp/routing-exp (PHASE_B_RESULTS.md, GATE_I_VERDICT.md, i-cells-final.json). Related: [[128bed35]] (v4.1), memory model-routing-convention.
